metadata.seed: Not really. I mean, it's pretty much the same pace. I'm very frustrated with the whole system. I'm working with inner city kids, african american kids, males in particular. And, you know, I sit and talk with these kids and I realize that there's not much I can do for them. You know, for the most part, we're talking about, you know, criminal arrest, drug abuse, you know, no education. And, you know, I don't know what I can really do for them anymore. I mean, you know, I sit and talk to them, but really, in terms of their statistics, it's looking pretty bad for them. So, you know, I get pretty frustrated and it's kind of depressing to do this every day.

patient.query: Not really. I mean, it's pretty much the same pace. I'm very frustrated with the whole system. I'm working with inner city kids, african american kids, males in particular. And, you know, I sit and talk with these kids and I realize that there's not much I can do for them. You know, for the most part, we're talking about, you know, criminal arrest, drug abuse, you know, no education. And, you know, I don't know what I can really do for them anymore. I mean, you know, I sit and talk to them, but really, in terms of their statistics, it's looking pretty bad for them. So, you know, I get pretty frustrated and it's kind of depressing to do this every day.

automatic_thoughts: there's not much I can do for them
it's looking pretty bad for them

alternative_perspective.response:
FINAL RESPONSE:

safety_check.response: It sounds like you're carrying a heavy burden, feeling stuck in a system that seems unresponsive to the challenges these kids face. I want to acknowledge how overwhelming that must be to witness these statistics and still feel responsible for their well-being.

patient.query: Yeah, it does feel pretty heavy sometimes. I think what frustrates me the most is feeling stuck at work, like I’m not really moving forward. I see others advancing, and it makes me question if I’m just... not good enough.

Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

patient.query: Yeah, I guess it just creeps in unexpectedly. Sometimes, I’ll be working on a project, and I’ll suddenly feel this wave of anxiety, like I’m just not cut out for it. Then I start second-guessing every little decision I make, and it’s exhausting.

patient.query: It’s like this voice in my head that whispers doubts every time I hit a bump in the road. When things don’t go as planned or I see someone else doing better, it just reinforces that feeling. I start thinking, “Maybe I should’ve seen this coming,” or “Why can’t I just keep up?” It’s frustrating to feel that way, especially when I want to succeed.

triggers: hitting a bump in the road
automatic_thoughts: Maybe I should’ve seen this coming
Why can’t I just keep up?

It makes me feel so anxious, like I’m just waiting for someone to point out my flaws. Some days, it just takes all the joy out of what I do.

someone will point out my flaws

But then I think, “What if I fail?” and it’s like I’m right back at square one with those feelings of self-doubt.
